notes: Follow-up to .07: fixed the one-time expense detail readability. WHAT WAS BROKEN: expanding a month's one-time cell rendered the per-item breakdown INSIDE the narrow month column, so expense names were truncated to a single letter (e.g. 'D', 'L...') and were unreadable, and it was worse on mobile. WHAT THE FIX DOES: tapping a one-time cell total now opens a floating detail popover rendered at document.body level with position:fixed, so it escapes the projection table's horizontal-scroll clipping and is width-capped to the viewport (min(280px, viewport-24)). The popover shows a header ('One-time · <month>'), each expense with its FULL name (wraps, no truncation) and amount sorted largest-first, and a Total row. It anchors under the tapped cell, clamps within the viewport, and flips above the cell if there isn't room below. It closes on a second tap of the same cell, on outside click, or on Escape. The previous inline .ot-detail/.ot-line approach and the _toggleOneTimeCell handler were removed. Per-item data is passed via a small window._otRegistry keyed by column, rebuilt each render. Scope remains display-only: no projection/net-worth/savings-chain/retirement math touched; the combined one-time row still sums the same per-month engine values. KNOWN ITEM (still deferred): current-month savings forecast uses the engine's PLANNED surplus; touches projectSavingsChain and needs double-count verification. NOTE carried forward: Dashboard Liquidity-health card uses p.cashSeries while the hero Liquidity value uses chain-adjusted cash; unify later if they diverge. SELF_TEST.js and PROPAGATION_TEST_{1,2,3}.js remain valid.
